articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

article.headline: France fight back to claim home win over Chile
article.description: France rallied from an early deficit to beat Chile 3-2 thanks to goals from Youssouf Fofana, Randal Kolo Muani and Oliver Giroud in a friendly at the Stade Velodrome on Tuesday.
article.linkText: France fight back to claim home win over Chile

article.story:
France rallied from an early deficit to beat Chile 3-2 thanks to goals from Youssouf Fofana, Randal Kolo Muani and Oliver Giroud in a friendly at the Stade Velodrome on Tuesday.

The visitors' early pressure paid off as they opened the scoring after six minutes with a low shot from Marcelino Núñez following a fine passing move from the right.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, NWSL more (U.S.)

But Fofana equalised for France in the 18th minute when Kylian Mbappé set him up on the edge of the box to fire past goalkeeper Claudio Bravo off a deflection from the defence.

"We got that first goal [equaliser] early on in the game and it was good to come back, which we weren't able to do against Germany," France boss Didier Deschamps told TF1 referring to Saturday's 2-0 loss to Germany.

"These are friendly matches and even though it's the French team our opponents put in a lot of energy and we had less. It's good to win but some of the things we did were a bit hard work."

Kolo Muani put the hosts in front with a close-range header from Theo Hernández's pinpoint cross in the 26th minute before Giroud sealed the win in the 72nd, extending his tally as France's all-time top scorer to 57 goals.

"Pretty tight game. We were determined to respond after the match against Germany," said Kolo Muani. "We were able to step up both offensively and defensively.

"Every match is important but above all it's a collective effort. It's all good if I'm on the pitch but everyone showed their class today."

The South Americans pulled one back eight minutes from time through Darío Osorio's long-range strike but could not prevent their first defeat under new coach Ricardo Gareca.

"I think we played a very solid game, we competed at the highest level and I think that's what we have to take," Chile captain Bravo told ESPN.

"We have the players, we have the capability and I think this game was very useful to measure what we can do. In general terms we're happy."

keyEvents.text: Goal!  France 0, Chile 1. Marcelino Núñez (Chile) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Mauricio Isla.
Substitution, France. Jules Koundé replaces Jonathan Clauss because of an injury.
Goal!  France 1, Chile 1. Youssouf Fofana (France) left footed shot from outside the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Kylian Mbappé.
Goal!  France 2, Chile 1. Randal Kolo Muani (France) header from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Théo Hernández with a cross.
Substitution, France. Mattéo Guendouzi replaces Eduardo Camavinga because of an injury.
Substitution, Chile. Ben Brereton replaces Eduardo Vargas.
Goal!  France 3, Chile 1. Olivier Giroud (France) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal. Assisted by Randal Kolo Muani.
Substitution, Chile. Esteban Pavez replaces Rodrigo Echeverría.
Substitution, Chile. Marcos Bolados replaces Alexis Sánchez.
Goal!  France 3, Chile 2. Darío Osorio (Chile) left footed shot from outside the box to the bottom right corner.
Substitution, France. Moussa Diaby replaces Randal Kolo Muani.
Substitution, France. Marcus Thuram replaces Olivier Giroud.
Substitution, Chile. Nicolás Fernández replaces Mauricio Isla.
Substitution, Chile. Matías Catalán replaces Igor Lichnovsky.
Substitution, Chile. César Pérez replaces Marcelino Núñez.
